ocr.text: As the Asia-Pacific real estate market continues to evolve, various trends have emerged that illustrate the dynamic interplay
between rental rates, co-working space utilization, and data center lease demand. In major urban centers such as Beijing and
Shanghai, the rental rate growth has shown distinct patterns, reflecting both local economic conditions and broader regional
shifts. For instance, in Beijing, we observe a notable percentage change in rental rates, highlighting a robust demand for
residential and commercial properties amidst increasing urbanization.
Market  RentalGrowth(%)  AvgRent(CNY)  Year-over-Year(%)  OccupancyRates  P/ERatio
Beijing  5.2  10,500  3.8  95%  18.5  Shanghai
Additionally, the co-working space utilization metric indicates a significant shift in work culture, with a recorded 95%
occupancy rate in select locations, underscoring the growing preference for flexible work environments in major metropolitan
areas.

Furthermore, data center lease demand remains strong, driven by the exponential growth of digital services and cloud
computing, with total leasing activity showing sustained upward momentum throughout the quarters. This report aims to
provide a comprehensive overview of these metrics, facilitating informed decision-making for investors and stakeholders
engaged in the Asia-Pacific real estate sector.
